classes.reasoning: This document is an editorial published in the journal OncoImmunology (2023, VOL. 12, NO. 1, 2175506) with DOI https://doi.org/10.1080/2162402X.2023.2175506. It is a peer-reviewed journal article discussing tumor-infiltrating lymphocyte therapy for melanoma, not a trial artifact or TMF document.

intervention: Tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs), ipilimumab (anti-CTLA-4), pembrolizumab (anti-PD-1), nivolumab (anti-PD-1), dabrafenib (BRAF inhibitor), trametinib
summary: An editorial article published in OncoImmunology journal discussing the use of tumor-infiltrating lymphocytes (TILs) for melanoma immunotherapy. The authors review recent clinical trial results comparing TIL therapy with ipilimumab in advanced melanoma patients and discuss the potential of personalized autologous TIL therapy.
references_to: Rohaan et al. N Engl J Med. 2022;387(23):2113–2125
Eggermont AMM et al. Nat Rev Clin Oncol. 2022;19(7):431–439
Robert C et al. N Engl J Med. 2015;372(26):2521–2532
Rosenberg SA et al. Clin Cancer Res. 2011;17(13):4550–4557
Fisher B et al. J Clin Oncol. 1989;7:250–261
Topalian SL et al. J Immunol Methods. 1987;102(1):127–141
Chandran SS et al. Lancet Oncol. 2017;18(6):792–802
Betof Warner A et al. Clin Cancer Res. 2022
